_meta.description: HAR-sourced reason enums for Stage 4 prod-transmitter writes. The transmitter never hard-codes a reason id — it always exact-string-matches the configured text against a live-fetched (or injected, for tests) reason list, and aborts the visit if no match is found. These 'selected' blocks are only the *default* text to look up; a real run must still resolve id/text from the live GET.
_meta.sourceHar: central pet shifts.json (captured 2026-07-09T14:54-15:01Z, visit 27000510, shift 44390825, store 391 scheduled / 215 decoded)
_meta.confirmedHar: James FM53 first-time complete sas-har-20260715-180316 (visit 27000977, shift 44392384) — time_change_reason id 5 + spent_time_reason >5% gate
_meta.signOff: 2026-07-13: T confirmed use of the real prod string in place of the originally-requested 'supervisor was notified' (which does not exist in any SAS reason enum). 2026-07-15: temporary over-hour demo on 24m visit confirmed duration≤work + spent_time_reason path.

categorySpentTimeReason.note: Original ask was 'supervisor was notified' (exact string) — no such reason exists in prod. Id 3 'Other – supervisor was contacted' is the closest match AND the one actually applied in this real visit's HAR when the category's cumulative spent time exceeded the 5% threshold (see failedAttemptHarEntry). Note this reason text also carries an en dash (U+2013), not a hyphen.
categorySpentTimeReason.failedAttemptMessage: Please select spent time reason as cummulative spent time on this category is more than 5.00%

shiftTimeChangeReason.note: Applied to time_change_reason on both shift PATCH calls (start-time entry and stop-time correction) in the HAR, alongside a free-text time_change_comment (HAR value was the placeholder 'k' — the assembler requires a real opts.timeChangeComment, it will not default to 'k').
